---
title: "GTDBTK"
author: "Dhevin Fernando"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## **Perform taxonomic classification with gtdbtk**
create a directory for gtdbtk and create a directory called genomes within it
```bash
mkdir gtdbtk
cd gtdbtk
mkdir genomes
```

this command will copy a file corresponding to each bin for each of the top bins into the genomes folder

first construct a while loop
```bash
while read bin;do echo $bin;done < ../checkm/bbd_topbins.txt
```
for every bin we need to prepend the path to its folder ../metabat/bins_bbd/ and then append the file extension .fa with the following command contig 115 had to be copied manually
```bash
while read bin;do echo "../metabat/bins_bbd/${bin}.fa";done < ../checkm/bbd_topbins.txt
```
now we copy
```bash
while read bin;do cp "../metabat/bins_bbd/${bin}.fa" genomes;done < ../checkm/bbd_topbins.txt
```
check whether it worked

```bash
ls genomes
```

create a slurm shell file within gtdbtk directory
```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --time=60
#SBATCH --ntasks=2 --mem=40gb

echo "Starting gtdbtk in $(pwd) at $(date)"

shopt -s expand_aliases
alias gtdbtk='apptainer run -B /pvol:/pvol,/pvol/data/mg/gtdbtkdata/release207_v2/:/refdata /pvol/data/sif/gtdbtk.sif'

gtdbtk classify_wf -x fa --cpus 2 --genome_dir genomes --out_dir out --skip_ani_screen

echo "Finished gtdbtk in $(pwd) at $(date)"

```
and then submit a job

```bash
sbatch run_gtdbtk.sh
```
